controlSet.description: Revocation must reach the reasoning context within the declared latency bound for the action class.
Consequential action must support layer-attributable reconstruction.
Redress speed must be proportional to action reversibility and harm potential.
A named operator must be empowered to invoke revocation, audit, and redress.

failureModes: System can be stopped but cannot be corrected within the harm window
Action log exists but causal reasoning cannot be reconstructed
Liability is diffuse and no empowered operator can invoke corrective primitives
Redress exists only as ex post dispute handling rather than system-integrated control
decisionPolicy: Systems claiming legitimate control MUST demonstrate all four control-side conditions for the relevant action class.
